steps.title: Find c from the given condition p(c) = 10
steps.content:
Substitute $x = c$ into the function definition: $$p(c) = \frac{(c-c)^2 + 160}{2c} = \frac{0 + 160}{2c} = \frac{80}{c}$$ Since we know $p(c) = 10$, set up the equation: $$\frac{80}{c} = 10$$ Multiply both sides by $c$ and divide by 10: $$80 = 10c$$
$$c = 8$$

steps.title: Calculate p(12) using c = 8
steps.content: Now substitute $x = 12$ and $c = 8$ into the original function: $$p(12) = \frac{(12-8)^2 + 160}{2(8)}$$ Simplify the numerator: $$p(12) = \frac{4^2 + 160}{16} = \frac{16 + 160}{16} = \frac{176}{16} = 11$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
